feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Business Application Studio
feed.subtitle: SAP Business Application Studio Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: List Control “Entity Set” pop up button missing
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I want to create an SAP Fiori App using SAP Business Application Studio, but "Select Entity Set" pop up missing. 

 

Regards!

Dan

entry.title: How to turn off the 'Add Comments | Refactor' automatically appearing line in the BAS Editor?
entry.content:
Hi Colleagues,

I'm using BAS, and whenever i select a variable/line in the code editor, automatically an 'Add Comment | Refactor' option appears above the selection, right in the middle of the code, which pushes the entire code down by a line, which, to be diplomatic, is extremely annoying.

I tried to turn off any AI Assistant related settings but could not figure out, how to get rid of these automatically appearing in line 'helping' features.

Do any of you have any ideas? The feature i'm talking about is this:

Every time you select something, this appear above, which moves the entire code.

Thank You and Best Regards,
Mihaly

entry.title: Cannot connect to a service - Build code to SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hello, I am developing an extension in SAP Build Code, and when trying to add a SSFF service, it does not appear as available.

 

 

The BTP Trial account has already been registered in the SSFF product, and the destination has been created.

 

 

As mentioned in the help documentation, it seems that this tool is not available when using the Full-Stack Application with the Productivity Tools dev space.

 

 

I would like to know if there is another way to add a SuccessFactors service in SAP Build Code within a Full-Stack Application project using the Productivity Tools dev space.

Has anyone else experienced the same issue?
Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Unable to access user details in UI5 application in BAS hosted on BTP cloud foundry.
entry.content:
Hi All,
I am trying to get details of user who as logged in to the application which is hosted on BTP cloud foundry.
I am using BAS for development.
I have used this code for this:

_fetchUserInfo: function () {
        fetch("/user-api/userinfo")
          .then(response => {
            if (!response.ok) {
              throw new Error("Failed to fetch user info");
            }
            return response.json();
          })
          .then(userInfo => {
            console.log("Logged-in user info:", userInfo);
            MessageToast.show("Welcome, " + userInfo.user_name);
          })
          .catch(error => {
            console.error("Error fetching user info:", error);
          });
      }

I have added the user api in xs-app.json as well:

"routes":[
{
      "csrfProtection": false,
      "source": "^/user-api(.*)",
      "target": "$1",
      "service": "sap-approuter-userapi",
      "authenticationType": "xsuaa"
    } 
]

After deploying the application to BTP I am getting error 404 not found.

Should I add this
this.getOwnerComponent().getManifestObject().resolveUri(sPath); in url

Please let me know how i can fix this issue.

entry.title: Not able to run MDK application through run as local web app option
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are working on MDK application using OData service, while running the application on Your Dev Space (run as local web app) option in Business Application studio, we are facing some issues as mentioned in below screenshot:

This error not only belongs to that particular app, we are facing this one for others apps also.

Along with it when we try to run the app on Mobile Svcs app in Android we are not able to fetch data and getting this error:

 

 

Any help would be appreciated on this.

@bill_froelich 

Thanks and Regards,

Madhav

entry.title: SAPUI5 how to filter at column level
entry.content:
Hello,

I am trying to develop an fiori application for SAP S/4 HANA using BTP Business application studio.

Product and Product Description has 1:n relation.

I have two columns in one table and I would like to display both Product ID and Description (filtered language eq English) in one table.

I think I have to use the filter. But how can I apply the filter only to one column? I want to use the filter something like this.  {path:'to_Description',parameters:{select:'Description'}, filters: [{path: 'Language', operator: 'EQ', value1: 'EN'}]}

Below is how my table is which is not displaying description. My goal is to displaying description.

 

<Table id="table" width="auto" items="{path: '/Material', parameters: {expand: 'to_Description'} }" noDataText="{worklistView>/tableNoDataText}" busyIndicatorDelay="{worklistView>/tableBusyDelay}" growing="true" growingScrollToLoad="true" updateFinished=".onUpdateFinished">

                <columns>
                    <Column id="materialColumn">
                        <Text text="Product ID" id="materialColumnTitle"/>
                    </Column>
                    <Column id="descriptionColumn">
                        <Text text="Description" id="descriptionColumnTitle"/>
                    </Column>
                </columns>

                <items>
                    <ColumnListItem type="Navigation" press=".onPress" id="test3">
                        <cells>
                            <Text text="{Material}"/>
                            <Text text="{to_Description/ProductDescription}" id="test2"/>
                        </cells>
                    </ColumnListItem>
                             
                </items>
            </Table>

entry.title: How to use nested $filter inside $expand while working with projection functions, CQL, OData?
entry.content:
Hello team,
I have following Odata query which works fine in Postman. Now I have to implement same logic in CAP.
.com/odata/v4/OPPS/BusinessUnitAssignments?$filter=(businessUnitID eq '1234')&$expand=promotion($filter=statusCode eq 'AC' and filterBuId eq 'CZP1%7C2B';$expand=promotionPriceDerivationRules($select=priceDerivationRuleID)) 

Here we need to expand promotion but only include promotions that match statusCode='AC' and filterBuId='CZP1|2B'

As you can see in query, we have to use 2 times expand, hence joined query with "semicolon". But in first expand we must use $filter, but in the beginning of query, we have already used $filter.
In CAP as we know, $filter == where()

Are there any limitations in .column() projection while doing filtering inside expansions?
How to use .where() clause in this situation?
Please support. Thanks.
Ex- This below snippet examples works fine but now how to use statusCode and filterBuId

            const results = await remote.run(
                SELECT.from(BusinessUnitAssignments)
                    .columns(b => {
                        b.businessUnitID,
                        b.promotion(p => {
                            p.promotionID,
                            p.promotionPriceDerivationRules(r => {
                                r.promotionPriceDerivationRuleID
                            })
                        })
                    })
                    .where({
                        businessUnitID: CustomerID,
                    })
            );

@vvdries @gregorw @qmacro

entry.title: SAP CAP - How to Set Attribute Type as Integer When Mocking User in package.json?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm currently mocking a user in my package.json for local testing in a CAP project, like this:

 

"gabriel": {
  "password": "@Teste123",
  "roles": ["AMS"],
  "attr": {
    "ams": "1"
  }
}

In the restrict rule, I need to compare the ams attribute as an integer:

{
  grant: ['READ'],
  to   : ['FORNECEDOR', 'TRANSPORTADOR', 'AMS'],
  where: '( CriadoPor = $user or Transportadora = $user ) or $user.AMS = 1'
}

This works fine in the deployed environment, where xs-security.json defines the ams attribute like this:

 
"attributes": [
  {
    "name": "ams",
    "description": "ams",
    "valueType": "int"
  }
]

However, during local development, the mocked ams attribute is always treated as a string (e.g., "1"), and the comparison with the integer 1 in the where clause fails.

Is there a way to define the type of a mocked attribute as int in package.json, or any workaround to handle this locally?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: SAP Application for Android/IOS Devices
entry.content:
Hello All,

I would like to know the opinion from Community members on building Fiori app for Android/IOS Devices. 

So far, explored the concepts of SAP Mobile Services with SAP Build(MDK Application) and SAP Business Application Studio from #https://developers.sap.com/mission.mobile-dev-kit-get-started.html

SAP Mobile Start - #https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/mobile-start-access-launchpad-site.html

Any other suggestions on building native apps using SAPUI5 library?

Thanks,

Muthu.

entry.title: Error Message when deploying from Business Application Studio
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I am new with the the Business Application Studio (and BTP spaces) and I am currently going through the example for the POD View Plugin for SAP DM Plugins (https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-digital-manufacturing/pod-plugin-developer-s-guide/view-pod-plugin-template-and-example?locale=en-US). When deploying the code the following error message is displayed:

I have made the name of our BTP account unrecognizable. This was in the blackened place as the organization.

I think to solve this error something in BTP needs to be adjusted, but I am not sure. Does anyone know what I need to do to solve the error?

Thanks!

entry.title: Date Issue in BAS
entry.content:
In BAS, I called API_INBOUND_DELIVERY_SRV Odata services and show data in table.

When I test in SOAP UI, DeliveryDate field value is shown correctly as <d:DeliveryDate>2025-05-09T00:00:00</d:DeliveryDate>. But in BAS, it shows as Thu May 08 2025 06:30:00 GMT+0630 (Myanmar Time).

How to get DeliveryDate value as SOAP UI in BAS?

Best Regards,

Khaing Zin

entry.title: SAP BAS FreeStyle app Adaptation Project , Extension Project
entry.content:
I am overriding  a method, when I try to access variables from the original controller

this.base.<variableName> , it is not returning any values.

 

sap.ui.define(
    [
        'sap/ui/core/mvc/ControllerExtension',
        'sap/ui/core/mvc/OverrideExecution'

    ],
    function (
        ControllerExtension, OverrideExecution
    ) {
        'use strict';
        return ControllerExtension.extend("customer.approverinbox.customS3", {
                       override: {
                <overrideMethod>: function (e, o) {
             let value =    this.base.<variable Name>;
                   
        });
    }
);

entry.title: "Could Not Load Content" Error in DevTools After BAS Deployment
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

When I run a UI5 application deployed through the BAS IDE and try to inspect the code in DevTools > Sources, I get the following error for any controller I select:

Could not load content for https://xxxx.xxxx.net:40030/sap/bc/ui5_ui5/sap/appName/~20250506134842~/controller/warehouseList-dbg.controller.js 
(HTTP error: status code 404, net::ERR_HTTP_RESPONSE_CODE_FAILURE)

Interestingly, if I made a change in a specific controller before deployment, the error doesn't appear for that one controller, only for the others.

I'm not sure if this is related to deployment settings or something else. I've never encountered this issue when deploying from SAP Web IDE.

Any suggestions or insights would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks



Additional info:
- The deployed application is being run by direct link.
- It is deployed using npm in the terminal.

ui5-deploy.yaml

# yaml-language-server: $schema=https://sap.github.io/ui5-tooling/schema/ui5.yaml.json

specVersion: "3.1"
metadata:
  name: appName.appName
type: application
builder:
  resources:
    excludes:
      - /test/**
      - /localService/**
  customTasks:
    - name: deploy-to-abap
      afterTask: generateCachebusterInfo
      configuration:
        target:
          destination: GWD
          url: http://xxxx.xxxx.net:40030
          client: '020'
        app:
          name: ZappName
          description: Test.
          package: ZGW
          transport: GWDKxxxxx
        exclude:
          - /test/

package.json

{
  "name": "appName.appName",
  "version": "0.0.1",
  "description": "",
  "devDependencies": {
    "@sap/ui5-builder-webide-extension": "1.1.x",
    "@sap/ux-ui5-tooling": "1",
    "@ui5/cli": "^4.0.12",
    "rimraf": "6.0.1"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "build_old": "ui5 build --clean-dest --include-task=generateManifestBundle generateCachebusterInfo",
    "start": "fiori run --open \"test/flpSandbox.html?sap-client=020&sap-ui-xx-viewCache=false#app-tile\"",
    "start-local": "fiori run --config ./ui5-local.yaml --open \"test/flpSandboxMockServer.html?sap-client=020&sap-ui-xx-viewCache=false#app-tile\"",
    "start-noflp": "fiori run --open \"index.html?sap-client=020&sap-ui-xx-viewCache=false\"",
    "start-variants-management": "fiori run --open \"preview.html?sap-client=020&sap-ui-xx-viewCache=false&fiori-tools-rta-mode=true&sap-ui-rta-skip-flex-validation=true#preview-app\"",
    "build": "ui5 build -a --clean-dest --include-task=generateCachebusterInfo",
    "deploy": "npm run build && fiori deploy --config ui5-deploy.yaml && rimraf archive.zip",
    "deploy-config": "fiori add deploy-config",
    "start-mock": "fiori run --open \"test/flpSandboxMockServer.html?sap-client=020&sap-ui-xx-viewCache=false#app-tile\"",
    "suite-tests": "fiori run --open 'test/testsuite.qunit.html'",
    "unit-tests": "fiori run --open 'test/unit/unitTests.qunit.html'",
    "int-tests": "fiori run --open 'test/integration/opaTests.qunit.html'",
    "undeploy": "npm run build && fiori undeploy --config ui5-deploy.yaml",
    "deploy-test": "npm run build && fiori deploy --config ui5-deploy.yaml --testMode true"
  },
  "ui5": {
    "dependencies": [
      "@sap/ui5-builder-webide-extension"
    ]
  },
  "private": true,
  "sapuxLayer": "CUSTOMER_BASE",
  "keywords": [
    "ui5",
    "openui5",
    "sapui5"
  ],
  "main": "webapp/index.html",
  "dependencies": {
    "deploy-config": "^1.0.5"
  }
}

entry.title: Need a step by step guide to build a Simple CAPM project with nodejs
entry.content:
Hi,

I need a step by step guide to create a CAPM project in (Business Application Studio) with nodejs runtime and a simple fiori app and a simple hana database (employee) with 3 columns (emp_number, Name, Dept)

I created one app , I was able create the  table in hana db, but failed in deploying the mtar file .
please help, we dont have complete guide on the internet.

Thanks

entry.title: Retrieving deleted files in SAP mobile development project in BAS
entry.content:
Retrieving deleted files in SAP mobile development project in BAS:

Hi Experts,

I had by mistakenly chose discard changes in the GIT version of BAS project.

This has deleted all the files from my project in the workspace.

Is there any way i can retrieve them.

 

Regards,

Ramnath Vivek

entry.title: Failed to get application list from Mobile Services with error: Request failed with status code 404.
entry.content:
Hi,

I had created a small MDK application in my BTP environment. In SAP Business Application Studio, I could use the deployment and the button to get the onboard QR code without any problems. Since a few days, connection seems to be "lost" between SAP BAS and the Mobile services. When I use Deploy, select "Mobile Services run as a mobile app", I need to select an application for mobile services. However, the list is empty. When pressing for the QR code, I've got this error:

In my BTP subaccount, I still have the subscriptions:

And also the entitlements for the mobile service:

Also on the Mobile Services page, I can see my application and the SAP Build page is showing it. I open my application in SAP BAS from this page.

I still can use the app on my mobile device. It's just that I cannot deploy new versions or get the onboarding QR. I must be overlooking something stupid. Can someone please advice?

entry.title: Error during request to remote service: Request failed with status code 404
entry.content:
How to resolve below error:

{
    "error": {
        "code": "502",
        "message": "Error during request to remote service: Request failed with status code 404"
    }
}

Background:
I have CAP NodeJS service which gets the data from remote/external service.
I am using XSUAA, OAuth2.0ClientCredentials authentication method for this service.
Also, mta.yaml is used for deployment on Cloud Foundry, where destination service and xsuaa service are configured.
It works as expected locally but but when I deployed on Cloud Foundry, it gives above error.

To configure hybrid/local testing, I have followed this documentation.
https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/using-services#connect-to-remote-services-locally

I am not sure the application has any conflict between service keys and bindings which I manually created to test real data on external service.
Can .cdsrc-private.json create any issue because I deployed into cloud foundry?
Is that fine when I have production profile and hybrid profile in CDS requires section in package.json file?

Tried to remove hybrid profile and rename of .cdsrc-private.json file and redeploy but still could not fix the issue.
Can anyone support on this?
Thanks.

package.json:

mta.yaml

Willem_Pardaens @vvdries @gregorw @qmacro

Reference:

Solved: Error during request to remote service: Failed to ... - SAP Community

Error during request to remote service: Failed to ... - SAP Community

entry.title: Is it common to use SAP BAS Productivity Tools in real business cases for CAP developement?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm currently following the SAP Learning Journey "Developing Full-Stack Applications Using Productivity Tools in SAP Business Application Studio". I find the tools quite intuitive and useful in a learning context, however, based on my experience across a few real-world CAP projects, I’ve rarely (if ever) seen these tools actually used in practice. Most teams I've worked with tend to rely on manual coding without touching the productivity features.

I'd really appreciate hearing about your experience or your team’s approach, especially if you've used these tools in production environments.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Fiori Elements: Change Column width
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

 

what is the easiest way to change the column width in a Fiori Elements app? In BAS there is a function to change the column width, but it does not seem to adjust it.

regards,

Lars

entry.title: Logout functionality SAP CAP application deployed in Cloud Foundry using Managed approuter
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I have built a SAP CAP application with UI5 and used managed approuter. The application is deployed in Cloud Foundry and I can access it through HTML5 applications. This application uses XSUAA authentication. 

I want to implement logout functionality in this application for the user. I have read many blogs and I'm unable to implement it. I have done things like making changes in xs-app.json file to include the logout parameter as described in the link:

https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/logout

On the logout button in my app, i put the controller logic to 

window.location.replace("/my/logout").

After this when I click on logout, it takes me to a URL:
"https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/resources/sap-ui-logout-url" and when i click the back button, it again goes directly into the application logged in as the previous user, which means that it is not deleting the session maybe.

I even followed the question:
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/logout-sap-ui5-app-cloud-foundry-sample-code/qaq-p/12248689

(Yes, I gave relative path in the controller script like my/logout as I am using managed approuter)

But when i do this, i get an internal server error after clicking on logout.

On checking the error logs of the app, I see the error as:
GET request to /74cf1c07-1827-49d2-9bc9-90ba4d9f6781.capleavemanagementappservice.leavemanagement-1.0.0/my/logout completed with status 500 request method (GET) does not match logout configuration logoutMethod (POST)


Does anybody know how can I implement a logout functionality in my application?

Thanks,
Raihan
